Client 2: And I want to make sure the month-to-month stays stable. I don’t want surprises.
Advisor: Perfect—timeline and cash flow. Let me start broad and then we’ll drill in. Danie, Emily, what do you have as your total household gross income per year right now?
Client 1: All-in, we’ve got it at 151100 annually.
Advisor: Got it—thank you. And just so I’m not mixing sources, Danie, how much of that is yours versus Emily’s?
Client 1: Mine is 120750 a year from the business side.

Client 2: And Danie has another monthly bonus line—103250 annually. It’s weird, but that’s how it shows up.
Advisor: No, that’s helpful. I’ll treat them as monthly-paid bonuses with those annual totals. Switching to spending—Emily, you mentioned stability. What do you estimate your total monthly expenses are, all-in?
Client 2: We try to keep it around 5200 a month.
Advisor: Okay—5200 monthly. When you say expenses, does that include any debt payments—like car loans, credit cards, anything like that?
Client 1: No. Monthly debt payments total is 0. We don’t have loans outstanding right now.

Client 2: And I want to make sure the month-to-month stays stable. I don’t want surprises.

Client 1: No. Monthly debt payments total is 0. We don’t have loans outstanding right now.
Advisor: Great—clean slate on debt. Now I want to understand what you’ve built up. Roughly, what do you consider your investable assets total—so brokerage, retirement accounts, that kind of thing?
Client 1: The total investable assets number we’re using is 1019950.
Advisor: Thanks. Let’s break that down so I know what’s where. Do you have a joint taxable brokerage account?
Client 1: Yes—joint taxable brokerage, value is 578850.

Client 1: Yes, there’s a private markets piece held through insurance. That one is 49400.
Advisor: Okay. And then home—just to make sure we’re not forgetting the big asset—your primary residence value, what are you using?
Client 2: We have the house at 321950.
Advisor: Perfect. And you said no loans outstanding, so no mortgage balance to pair with that—correct?
Client 1: Correct, no mortgage. No debt.

Client 2: Two adults—2.

Advisor: Perfect—timeline and cash flow. Let me start broad and then we’ll drill in. Danie, Emily, what do you have as your total household gross income per year right now?

Advisor: Hi Danie, hi Emily—good to meet you both. I’m glad you made the time. Before we get into the weeds, what would make today’s meeting a win for you?
Client 1: Thanks. Honestly, I want to know if we’re on track to retire around 64 without doing anything dumb tax-wise.
Client 2: And I want to make sure the month-to-month stays stable. I don’t want surprises.

Client 2: Four—so 4 dependents.
Advisor: Perfect. Now, shifting to taxes for a minute—when you think about your marginal bracket, where are you landing these days?
Client 1: We’re in the 37% bracket.
Advisor: Okay. And just so I’m not mixing terms—when you say 37%, that’s the federal bracket you plan around, right?
Client 1: Yes, 37% federal is what we use for planning.

Client 2: No, it’s all US.
Advisor: Perfect. That’s all I needed on that section—thank you.
Advisor: Okay—thanks. Staying on the household basics for a second: Danie and Emily, are you two married, or is it a different arrangement I should reflect?
Client 2: We’re married.
Advisor: Got it. And you mentioned the kids earlier—how many dependents are we counting right now?

Advisor: Okay. And just so I’m not mixing terms—when you say 37%, that’s the federal bracket you plan around, right?
Client 1: Yes, 37% federal is what we use for planning.
Advisor: Great. Last piece in this section: how would you describe your overall investing comfort level—conservative, moderate, aggressive—what fits best?
Client 1: Moderate. I’m okay with some market movement, I just don’t want anything that could blow up our timeline.
Advisor: Yep, that makes sense. Emily, does “moderate” feel right to you too, or would you call it a little more cautious?

Advisor: Hi Danie, hi Emily—good to meet you both. I’m glad you made the time. Before we get into the weeds, what would make today’s meeting a win for you?
